|gate=$2,400,000<ref name="attendance/gate" />
|buyrate=300,000<ref>{{Cite tweet |user=jedigoodman |url= https://twitter.com/jedigoodman/status/1075780950875754496 |number=1075780950875754496 |title=From WON, the current estimates for... |access-date=2023-04-06}}</ref>

'''''UFC 231: Holloway vs. Ortega''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] held on December 8, 2018 at [[Scotiabank Arena]] in [[Toronto]], [[Ontario]], Canada.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2018/07/ufc-announces-2018-schedule-september-through-end-of-year|title=UFC announces rest of 2018 schedule from September through end of year|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2018-07-08|access-date=2018-07-09}}</ref>

== Background ==
A [[UFC Featherweight Championship]] bout between [[Max Holloway]] and [[Brian Ortega]] served as the event headliner.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2018/10/max-holloway-vs-brian-ortega-official-ufc-231|title=Max Holloway vs. Brian Ortega title fight officially back on at UFC 231 in Toronto|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2018-10-01|access-date=2018-10-01}}</ref> The fight was originally scheduled to take place at [[UFC 226]], but Holloway pulled out due to "concussion like symptoms".<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2018/07/max-holloway-withdraws-ufc-226-concussion-like-symptom|title=Max Holloway out of UFC 226, hospitalized with concussion-like symptoms|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2018-07-04|access-date=2018-07-04}}</ref>

In the co-headlining slot, a bout for the vacant [[UFC Women's Flyweight Championship]] between former [[UFC Women's Bantamweight Championship]] challenger [[Valentina Shevchenko]] and former [[UFC Women's Strawweight Championship|UFC Women's Strawweight Champion]] [[Joanna Jędrzejczyk]] took place. If victorious, Jędrzejczyk would become the first woman to win titles in different divisions.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.cbssports.com/mma/news/joanna-jedrzejczyk-vs-valentina-shevchenko-set-for-flyweight-title-fight-at-ufc-231/|title=Joanna Jedrzejczyk vs. Valentina Shevchenko set for flyweight title fight at UFC 231|publisher=cbssports.com|author=Brandon Wise|date=2018-09-20|access-date=2018-09-21}}</ref> They met previously in amateur muay thai and kickboxing, with Shevchenko winning all three encounters.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2016/07/valentina-shevchenko-has-3-wins-over-ufc-champ-joanna-jedrzejczyk-whos-now-a-supporter|title=Valentina Shevchenko has 3 wins over UFC champ Joanna Jedrzejczyk, who's now a supporter|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Justin Park|date=2016-07-23|access-date=2018-09-22}}</ref> On October 2, the UFC announced that Shevchenko would face [[Sijara Eubanks]] at [[UFC 230]] instead, as that event was in need of a headliner.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2018/10/ufc-230-main-event-valentina-shevchenko-vs-sijara-eubanks-vacant-flyweight-title-joanna-jedrzejczyk|title=UFC 230 finally has main event: Valentina Shevchenko vs. Sijara Eubanks for vacant flyweight title|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2018-10-02|access-date=2018-10-02}}</ref> The fight between Shevchenko and Jędrzejczyk was put back together a week later though, as a fight between then [[UFC Heavyweight Championship|UFC Heavyweight]] and [[UFC Light Heavyweight Championship|Light Heavyweight Champion]] [[Daniel Cormier]] and [[Derrick Lewis]] wound up filling the headlining slot at [[UFC 230]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/24943833/daniel-cormier-derrick-lewis-headline-ufc-230|title=Daniel Cormier-Derrick Lewis official; Valentina Shevchenko moved back to UFC 231|work=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto|date=2018-10-09|access-date=2018-10-09}}</ref>

A lightweight bout between [[Gilbert Burns]] and [[Olivier Aubin-Mercier]] was previously scheduled to take place in February 2018 at [[UFC on Fox: Emmett vs. Stephens]]. However, Burns was pulled from the pairing during the week leading up to the event after medical officials deemed that it was unsafe for Burns to cut the necessary weight for the 156-pound limit.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2018/2/21/17036504/gilbert-burns-vs-olivier-aubin-mercier-scratched-from-ufc-on-fox-28|title=Gilbert Burns vs. Olivier Aubin-Mercier scratched from UFC on FOX 28|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Shaun Al-Shatti|date=2018-02-21|access-date=2018-02-21}}</ref> The fight has been rescheduled for this event.<ref name="taketwo">{{cite web|url=http://mmadna.nl/poging-2-gilbert-burns-vs-olivier-aubin-mercier-tijdens-ufc-231-in-toronto/|title=Attempt 2: Gilbert Burns vs. Olivier Aubin-Mercier at UFC 231 in Toronto|publisher=mmadna.nl|author=Marcel Dorff|date=2018-08-22|access-date=2018-08-22|language=nl}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between [[The Ultimate Fighter: Brazil 3]] heavyweight winner [[Antônio Carlos Júnior]] and [[The Ultimate Fighter Nations: Canada vs. Australia|The Ultimate Fighter: Nations]] middleweight winner [[Elias Theodorou]] was initially scheduled to take place at [[UFC Fight Night: Santos vs. Anders]]. However, Júnior pulled out on August 28 due to injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2018/8/28/17793374/antonio-carlos-junior-out-of-ufc-sao-paulo-fight-against-elias-theodorou|title=Antonio Carlos Junior out of UFC Sao Paulo fight against Elias Theodorou|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Marc Raimondi|date=2018-08-27|access-date=2018-08-28}}</ref> The pairing was then rescheduled for this event.<ref name="Shoeface/Theodorou">{{cite web|url=http://mmabrasil.com.br/cara-de-sapato-se-machuca-e-luta-com-elias-theodorou-e-adiada-para-o-ufc-231|title=Cara de Sapato gets hurt and fights with Elias Theodorou is delayed for UFC 231|publisher=mmabrasil.com.br|author=Gabriel Camargo|date=2018-08-28|access-date=2018-08-29|language=pt|archive-date=2018-08-29|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180829212013/http://mmabrasil.com.br/cara-de-sapato-se-machuca-e-luta-com-elias-theodorou-e-adiada-para-o-ufc-231|url-status=dead}}</ref> In turn, on September 13, it was reported that Júnior was forced to withdraw as a surgery is required to treat the injury that caused the first cancellation of this bout.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2018/9/14/17859124/antonio-carlos-junior-says-hes-out-of-ufc-231-bout-with-elias-theodorou|title=Antonio Carlos Junior says he's out of UFC 231 bout with Elias Theodorou|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Danny Segura|date=2018-09-14|access-date=2018-09-14}}</ref> On October 1, it was reported that [[Eryk Anders]] would replace him.<ref name="twoadded">{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2018/10/jimi-manuwa-vs-thiago-marreta-santos-eryk-anders-vs-elias-theodorou-added-to-ufc-231|title=Jimi Manuwa vs. Thiago 'Marreta' Santos, Eryk Anders vs. Elias Theodorou added to UFC 231|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2018-10-01|access-date=2018-10-02}}</ref>

A light heavyweight bout between [[Jimi Manuwa]] and [[Thiago Santos]] was expected to headline [[UFC Fight Night: Santos vs. Anders]], but the bout was scrapped due to an injury suffered by Manuwa.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bloodyelbow.com/2018/9/16/17868434/mma-news-jimi-manuwa-injured-eryk-anders-vs-thiago-santos-ufc-sao-paulo-main-event|title=Jimi Manuwa injured, Eryk Anders now fights Thiago Santos in UFC Sao Paulo main event|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Mookie Alexander|date=2018-09-16|access-date=2018-10-02|archive-date=2018-09-17|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180917105224/https://www.bloodyelbow.com/2018/9/16/17868434/mma-news-jimi-manuwa-injured-eryk-anders-vs-thiago-santos-ufc-sao-paulo-main-event|url-status=dead}}</ref> The pairing took place at this event.<ref name="twoadded"/>

A featherweight bout between [[Renato Moicano]] and [[Mirsad Bektić]] was expected to take place at the event. However, Bektić was forced out of the bout due to an undisclosed injury on November 15.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2018/11/ufc-231-mirsad-bektic-injured-out-vs-renato-moicano|title=Mirsad Bektic out of UFC 231 bout vs. Renato Moicano due to injury|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2018-11-15|access-date=2018-11-16}}</ref> Moicano instead served as the backup fighter for the main event.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2018/11/ufc-231-renato-moicano-backup-holloway-vs-ortega|title=Renato Moicano named backup to Max Holloway-Brian Ortega title fight at UFC 231|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2018-11-28|access-date=2018-11-28}}</ref>

[[John Makdessi]] was expected to face [[Carlos Diego Ferreira]] at the event. However, Makdessi pulled out of the fight on November 28 and was replaced by returning veteran [[Jesse Ronson]].<ref name="RonsonIn">{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2018/11/28/18116334/jesse-ronson-john-makdessi-diego-ferreira-ufc-231|title=Jesse Ronson returns to the UFC, replaces John Makdessi vs. Diego Ferreira at UFC 231|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2018-11-28|access-date=2018-11-28}}</ref> However, on December 4, it was announced that Ronson was pulled from the bout after Ontario Athletics Commission officials deemed that he was too heavy to safely make the lightweight 156-pound limit.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2018/12/jesse-ronson-too-heavy-out-ufc-231-diego-ferreira|title=Jesse Ronson 'too heavy,' ruled out of UFC 231 bout vs. Diego Ferreira|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|date=2018-12-04|access-date=2018-12-05}}</ref> He was replaced by newcomer [[Kyle Nelson (fighter)|Kyle Nelson]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2018/12/5/18127500/diego-ferreira-draws-short-notice-replacement-kyle-nelson-for-ufc-231|title=Diego Ferreira draws short-notice replacement Kyle Nelson for UFC 231|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Alexander K. Lee|date=2018-12-05|access-date=2018-12-06}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters were awarded $50,000 bonuses:<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.com/2018/12/ufc-231-bonuses-max-holloway-gets-two-thiago-marreta-santos|title=UFC 231 bonuses: Max Holloway earns double after dominant title defense|date=2018-12-09|publisher=mmajunkie.com|author=Staff|access-date=2018-12-09}}</ref>
*''' Fight of the Night: Max Holloway vs. Brian Ortega'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Max Holloway''' and '''Thiago Santos'''
